name: Baklava Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 32 ounces cream cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Position the rack in the lower third of the oven.
Beat the cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Gradually mix in honey, then lemon juice, and vanilla extract. Beat in eggs one at a time until just incorporated. Set aside.
Brush a 10-inch springform pan with clarified butter. Arrange one phyllo sheet with the long edge parallel to the edge of the surface (cover other sheets with a dampened towel). Brush the left half of the sheet with butter and fold the right half over. Brush the top with butter. Place in the prepared pan, leaving a 5-inch overhang on one end. Cover with another dampened towel. Brush and fold the second sheet and arrange it in the pan, overlapping the first sheet by 3 inches. Repeat with four more sheets, covering the entire pan. Wrap any remaining phyllo in plastic and refrigerate.
Stir through the filling and pour into the crust. Cover the filling with the overhanging ends of phyllo at the edge of the pan. Bake until the pastry is lightly browned and the cake is firm to the touch, about 50 minutes. Remove the pan sides. Using a toothpick, poke 12 holes in the top of the cake to allow heat to escape. Cool completely on a rack. Refrigerate for 2 days to mellow flavors, covering after the first day.
For topping: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Cover a baking sheet with two sheets of parchment paper.
Coarsely grind all nuts (walnuts and almonds) with 1 tablespoon sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon.
Stack the 10 reserved phyllo pastry sheets on the work surface. Set the rim of the 10-inch springform pan atop the pastry stack. Cut around the inside of the rim through the entire stack using a sharp knife. Using a sharp knife, make ten rounds. Cover with a damp towel.
Set the pan rim on the prepared baking sheet. Brush the parchment and the inside of the pan rim with butter. Place one pastry round in the pan rim and brush with butter. Repeat with four more rounds. Spread the nut mixture evenly over the pastry. Top with the remaining five rounds, brushing each with butter. Using a ruler as a guide, cut the pastry into 16 wedges with a sharp knife. Sprinkle lightly with water. Bake until crisp and golden, about 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat 1/2 cup sugar, 1/4 cup water, and 1 teaspoon lemon juice in a small saucepan over low heat, swirling the pan occasionally until the sugar dissolves. Add the cinnamon stick, increase heat, and boil until syrupy, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat. When bubbles subside, stir in brandy if desired. Flatten the pastry atop the cake. Remove the pan rim from the topping. Set the topping on the cake, using a large spatula. Recut the wedges.
Replace the pan rim on the cake. Pour the hot syrup over the top. Cool for 1 hour. Refrigerate for 1 to 6 hours. Let the cake stand at room temperature for 20 minutes before serving.
